spec_match.rationale: The working demo correctly uses multiple background layers to show image(<color>) as a network fallback.

interactive.rationale: Simple but effective interactivity with a color picker updating the live previews and code block.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The parent blurb matches the concept of using image() as a fallback.

edge_cases.rationale: The code example in the pre block incorrectly suggests that placing image() inside image-set() works as a network fallback, which contradicts how image-set resolution works.

references.rationale: Includes the ChromeStatus entry reference link.

openQuestions.title: Fix fallback code example in gradient-fallback
openQuestions.detail: The code example shows `image()` inside `image-set()` as a fallback for network failures. However, `image-set()` resolution happens at style computation time, not network load time, so it will not fall back to the color if the URL fails to load. The demo itself correctly uses multiple background layers (`image-set(...), image(...)`) to achieve this. The code example should be updated to match the working multiple-background implementation to avoid misleading developers.

summary: A useful demo showing how to use solid-color images as fallbacks. However, it contains a misleading code example that suggests image-set() performs network-level fallbacks, whereas the actual demo correctly uses multiple background layers to achieve the effect. This should be corrected.
